title: Data Science / Product Manager Intern – GraphRAG (AI Q&A on Technical Docs)
description: Studied and optimized a GraphRAG pipeline leveraging knowledge graphs for maintenance-document Q&A. Integrated LangChain, MLflow, and GCP Vertex AI; benchmarked against IBM/HKU approaches. Outcomes: +50% model accuracy, +70% data accessibility. Wrote PRDs/demos, presented results to senior engineers, ensured scalability and business alignment.

details.subtitle: Apr–Aug 2025 · 5 months
details.intro: I worked as a Data Science / Product Management intern to design and evaluate a GraphRAG pipeline that answers questions on complex maintenance and technical documents. The system augments LLM retrieval with knowledge graphs to ground responses, reduce hallucinations, and make institutional knowledge easier to discover.

sections.content: I led discovery → PRD → evaluation loops with engineers and stakeholders, and integrated LangChain, MLflow, and GCP Vertex AI to operationalize experiments and track model performance. I benchmarked internal approaches against external references (e.g., IBM and HKU projects) to validate design choices and guide roadmap trade-offs.
list: Outcomes: +50% improvement in QA accuracy and +70% increase in data accessibility measured in internal evaluations and stakeholder demos.
RAG design: entity/relation extraction → graph construction → retrieval over subgraphs → LLM generation with grounded citations.
Evaluation: curated gold sets, exact-match/semantic metrics, error taxonomy, and failure-mode analysis to prioritize fixes.
Scalability: designed for incremental graph updates and cost-aware inference on Vertex AI; tracked experiments with MLflow.
Stakeholders: authored PRDs, ran live demos, and delivered English presentations to senior engineers and cross-functional teams.

details.conclusion: This project bridged user needs and engineering constraints: we translated ambiguous questions into reproducible retrieval tasks, instrumented quality and cost, and shipped a path to scale GraphRAG across additional document families and use cases.

title: Entrepreneur – Safe Hitchhiking Mobile App
description: Led discovery → UX → prototype for a safety-focused ride-sharing app. Ran field research and user interviews; defined MVP and trust features; designed intuitive UI to drive adoption. Managed delivery end-to-end with collaborators. Outcome: ~5% increase in local interest in hitchhiking; strengthened PM, UX, and go-to-market skills.

details.subtitle: Sep 2024 – Mar 2025
details.intro: Founded and led the development of a ride-sharing mobile application designed to promote safe and sustainable hitchhiking. Conducted market research and user interviews to identify key pain points in shared mobility and safety, translating findings into actionable product requirements.

sections.content: Designed and implemented the UX/UI for the prototype, integrating user feedback to enhance trust and usability. Built dashboards for business intelligence and engagement tracking to inform iterative product improvements. Managed the end-to-end lifecycle—from concept to pilot testing—achieving strong local traction and validating product-market fit.

details.conclusion: This entrepreneurial experience strengthened my skills in product management, user research, data-driven decision-making, and team leadership, while fostering an innovation mindset focused on delivering real-world impact.

title: Communications Lead – IMT Atlantique Gala 2025
description: Owned comms strategy for a 300+ attendee flagship event with a €100k budget. Launched multi-channel campaigns (posters, socials, newsletters). Outcomes: +45% social engagement, +30% newsletter open rate, +20% external ticket registrations, 95% satisfaction. Coordinated cross-functional teams and vendors.

details.subtitle: Jul 2024 – Feb 2025
details.intro: Led the communications and marketing strategy for the IMT Atlantique Gala 2025, the school’s flagship event gathering over 300 participants, including students, alumni, and corporate partners. Managed a €100,000 budget and oversaw all digital and print communication initiatives from concept to execution.

sections.content: Designed and launched multi-channel campaigns (social media, newsletters, posters, video teasers) that increased online engagement by 45% and newsletter open rates by 30% compared to the previous edition. Managed content calendars, branding, and visual identity to ensure message consistency and high audience engagement.

Coordinated a cross-functional team of designers, developers, and marketing volunteers to deliver the event website and promotional materials on schedule. Strengthened partnerships with sponsors and external stakeholders, driving a 20% increase in external ticket sales and a 95% satisfaction rate in post-event surveys.

details.conclusion: This role enhanced my expertise in communication strategy, stakeholder management, event marketing, and project leadership—skills directly transferable to product management and cross-functional coordination in tech environments.

title: Team Leader – Strategic Education Project
place: University of KwaZulu-Natal (South Africa) · IMT Atlantique
description: Led a team of 6; conducted 20+ interviews (students, managers) to identify future industry competencies. Built a strategic framework aligning programs with evolving tech/market needs. Outcomes: +25% employability readiness (post-project surveys), 3 program changes adopted; developed stakeholder management and research rigor.

details.subtitle: Sep 2024 – Jan 2025
details.intro: Led a mixed-methods study on future industrial skills, conducting 20+ interviews with students and industry managers and running targeted surveys to map emerging competency needs across sectors.

sections.content: Translated findings into a practical capability framework and recommendations adopted by partner institutions, aligning curricula with market demand across 6 universities in 5 countries. Outcomes: +25% employability readiness (post-project surveys) and implementation of 3 program changes. Responsibilities included scoping, stakeholder facilitation, insight synthesis, and presentation to academic and industry boards.

title: Engineering Intern – Operational Lead (VCS Relocation)
description: Led relocation and commissioning of a critical Voice Communication System (VCS). Redesigned technical spaces and ergonomics; ensured compliance and safety. Outcomes: ~15% operator efficiency improvement; produced comprehensive technical documentation and coordinated multi-disciplinary teams.

details.subtitle: Jun–Jul 2024 · 2 months
details.intro: Led the relocation and operational deployment of a Voice Communication System (VCS) within the DGAC’s control facilities, ensuring uninterrupted service continuity during transition.

sections.content: Collaborated with multidisciplinary engineering and operations teams to redesign the technical workspace, improving layout and ergonomics for controllers. The reorganization increased operator efficiency by approximately 15% and enhanced communication reliability across critical systems.

details.conclusion: Produced comprehensive technical documentation to support maintenance and knowledge transfer, ensuring long-term sustainability of the new setup. This experience strengthened my expertise in project management, process optimization, and systems engineering within a high-stakes, safety-critical environment.
